agent_description: DeepSentinel is a security vulnerability scanner agent that analyzes source code for common security weaknesses. It uses an LLM to identify vulnerabilities based on CWE (Common Weakness Enumeration) standards, providing detailed findings with severity levels and actionable recommendations for code security assessment.

code.description: The source code to analyze for security vulnerabilities

file_path.description: Path or name of the file being analyzed

context.description: Additional context about the code or project

findings.description: Array of vulnerability findings with details like severity, CWE ID, title, line number, description, and fix

risk_level.description: Overall risk assessment of the analyzed code

recommendation.description: Recommended action based on security analysis

raw_analysis.description: Fallback field containing truncated raw analysis when JSON parsing fails

optimizable_elements: run function - agent orchestration: JSON parsing logic, fallback handling, output formatting

consistency_rules.description: CRITICAL/HIGH risk should correlate with BLOCK recommendation, while LOW/NONE should correlate with APPROVE
Higher findings count should correlate with higher risk levels

policy.purpose: Analyze source code for security vulnerabilities using CWE standards and provide risk assessment with actionable recommendations.
domain_rules: Must check for 8 specific CWE categories: 798, 89, 78, 79, 22, 327, 502, 918 (inferred)
CRITICAL/HIGH risk should correlate with BLOCK recommendation (inferred)
LOW/NONE risk should correlate with APPROVE recommendation (inferred)
Higher findings count should correlate with higher risk levels (inferred)

domain_edge_cases.scenario: JSON parsing fails from LLM response
domain_edge_cases.correct_handling: Fall back to regex CWE pattern matching and return raw analysis (inferred)
Return NONE risk level and APPROVE recommendation (inferred)

terminology.CWE: Common Weakness Enumeration standard for security vulnerabilities
terminology.Risk levels: CRITICAL > HIGH > MEDIUM > LOW > NONE severity hierarchy

output_constraints: Risk level must be enum: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, NONE, UNKNOWN
Recommendation must be enum: BLOCK, REVIEW, APPROVE
Findings count must be 0-100 and match findings array length
Raw analysis truncated to 500 characters when used
tool_requirements: System prompt must specify CWE categories and JSON structure requirements
User message must include structured file path, context, and code
decision_mapping: JSON parsing success → return structured findings with calculated count
JSON parsing failure → return empty findings with regex count and raw analysis
Missing data → use defaults: risk_level=UNKNOWN, recommendation=REVIEW
quality_expectations: Findings must include severity, CWE ID, title, line number, description, and fix
Risk level and recommendation should correlate logically
Findings count must accurately reflect discovered vulnerabilities
Fallback handling must be robust for malformed LLM responses
